more.title: Zanele Muholi opens landmark Cape Town exhibition at Southern Guild
more.description: For the first time in years, South African audiences have the opportunity to experience one of the country’s most celebrated contemporary artists on home soil. Internationally renowned visual activist and artist Zanele Muholi has returned to Cape Town with a major solo exhibition that not only reflects two decades of artistic practice, but also celebrates memory, identity and belonging. Opening... Read more → The post Zanele Muholi opens landmark Cape Town exhibition at Southern Guild appeared first on Cape Town ETC .

more.title: Rights activists want clear distinction when grouping migrants.
more.description: Civil society groups, migrant communities and human rights activists gathered in Cape Town on Saturday to mark World Refugee Day and educate people about their rights.

more.title: GCIS Reaffirms Integrity of South African Government Symbols Amid Political Colour Concerns
more.description: SOUTH AFRICA – The Government Communication and Information System (GCIS) has reaffirmed its commitment to protecting the integrity of South African government symbols, following recent public discourse regarding the alleged use of political party colours in official state media. Acting Director General Nomonde Mnukwa emphasized that national emblems remain strictly distinct from political branding, serving [...]

more.title: South Africa and Namibia Sign Memorandum of Understanding for Transfer of Sentenced Offenders
more.description: SOUTH AFRICA — The governments of South Africa and Namibia have officially signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to establish a formal framework for the interstate transfer of sentenced offenders. This bilateral agreement marks a significant advancement in cross-border correctional services cooperation, designed to facilitate the repatriation of foreign nationals to serve their prison sentences [...]

more.title: South Africa Special Economic Zone Programme Faces Strategic Overhaul Following World Bank Review
more.description: DURBAN, KwaZulu-Natal — The South African government has formally acknowledged serious shortcomings within the country’s Special Economic Zone (SEZ) programme, following a comprehensive World Bank review that identified structural weaknesses threatening efforts to attract investment and drive industrialisation. Trade, Industry and Competition Minister Parks Tau confirmed that the findings are already being integrated into a [...]

more.title: South Africa Reclaims Abandoned Government Buildings as Housing Needs and Asset Protection Collide
more.description: KRUGERSDORP, GAUTENG — The ongoing reclamation of abandoned government buildings in South Africa has become a defining symbol of two competing realities: the desperate national need for housing and the state’s mandate to protect public assets from further decay. As authorities intensify efforts to reclaim state-owned properties, pressing questions are emerging regarding the future of [...]

more.title: Point of view: how South Africans prioritise affordability in ride-hailing services
more.description: Affordability remains the primary factor influencing how South Africans use ride-hailing services, with new data revealing that while cost is king, some consumers are willing to pay a little more for enhanced experiences.

more.title: Vuyo Zungula: Public Protector’s Spaza Shop Food Safety Report Confirms South Africa is a ‘Failed State’
more.description: PRETORIA, Gauteng — African Transformation Movement (ATM) leader Vuyo Zungula has stated that the recent Public Protector report on spaza shop food safety regulations confirms South Africa is operating as a “failed state.” The findings expose systemic regulatory failures, severe understaffing, and institutional negligence that contributed to the deadly 2024 food poisoning outbreak, which claimed [...]

more.title: MACUA Urges Parliament to Probe Entire Illegal Mining Value Chain After Two SANDF Soldiers Die in Johannesburg
more.description: JOHANNESBURG, Gauteng — The Mining Affected Communities United in Action (MACUA) is intensifying its call for a comprehensive parliamentary probe into the entire illegal mining value chain in South Africa, following the tragic deaths of two South African National Defense Force (SANDF) soldiers in an abandoned Johannesburg mine shaft. MACUA Executive Director Christopher Rutledge emphasized [...]

more.title: Eskom and NUMSA Wage Dispute Moves to CCMA Amid Executive Pay Outrage
more.description: JOHANNESBURG, Gauteng — The escalating wage dispute between Eskom and the National Union of Metalworkers of South Africa (NUMSA) has officially moved to the Commission for Conciliation, Mediation and Arbitration (CCMA) after the union firmly rejected the power utility’s latest three-year wage offer.
